region_of_origin: China (Imperial Chinese cuisine, adapted in Singapore)
food_subgroup: Deep-fried fish with sweet and sour sauce

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for chrysanthemum-shaped scored and deep-fried fish with sweet and sour sauce. FOODON_03302051 (fried fish food product) is the closest defensible parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Fish, fried) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for chrysanthemum-shaped scored deep-fried fish with sweet and sour sauce.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for this specific prepared fish dish.

main_ingredients: white fish fillet (typically grass carp or snapper)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of chrysanthemum-shaped crispy fish is approximately 300 g, representing a shared dish for 2–3 diners, consisting of a whole scored fish fillet deep-fried to a crispy flower shape and coated in sweet and sour sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Sweet and Sour Fish (糖醋鱼) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and adapted composition from FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table for deep-fried fish with sweet and sour sauce
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for chrysanthemum-shaped scored deep-fried fish with sweet and sour sauce exists in any approved primary source. Values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for deep-fried fish (NDB 15264, Fish, mixed species, cooked, dry heat) with standard sweet and sour sauce composition from USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 169238, Sauce, sweet and sour, ready-to-serve) in an approximate 70:30 fish-to-sauce ratio by weight, adjusted for oil absorption during deep-frying. This is a best-effort estimation; values should be verified against a laboratory analysis of the specific restaurant preparation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sweet and sour dishes — analogous estimate based on battered deep-fried fish with sweet and sour sauce; reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281–2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for chrysanthemum-shaped crispy fish with sweet and sour sauce. GI of 55 is estimated as a low-GI value based on the analogous category of battered deep-fried fish dishes combined with sweet and sour sauce (high sugar content, moderate starch from batter). The sweet and sour sauce contributes significant simple sugars (sucrose, glucose from ketchup and vinegar-sugar blend), while the cornstarch batter contributes moderate starch. A composite GI of approximately 55 (low) is defensible. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 48.0) / 100 = 26.4, where carbohydrate per 300 g serving = 16.0 × 3 = 48.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 380 mg per 100 g; a 300 g serving delivers approximately 1140 mg sodium, representing approximately 50% of the recommended daily limit of 2300 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 100–150 g and request reduced-sodium sauce preparation, as the sweet and sour sauce and soy sauce components contribute substantially to the sodium load of this dish.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (approximately 9.5 g sugar per 100 g) from the sweet and sour sauce, combined with a low glycemic index of approximately 55 and a glycemic load of 26.4 per standard 300 g serving.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this dish in small portions (100–150 g) and pair it with low-GI vegetables or high-fibre foods to blunt the postprandial glucose response driven by the sugar-rich sweet and sour sauce and cornstarch batter.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate fat content (8.5 g per 100 g) from deep-frying, with saturated fat at 1.4 g per 100 g; the fish base provides beneficial omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids (approximately 2.8 g PUFA per 100 g), partially offsetting cardiovascular risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease may consume this dish occasionally in moderate portions (100–150 g), benefiting from the omega-3 content of the fish, but should be mindful of the added sugar in the sauce and the oil absorbed during deep-frying, which increase total caloric and glycemic load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Crispy Chrysanthemum-Shaped Sliced Fish with Sweet and Sour Sauce (宫廷菊花鱼, Gong Ting Ju Hua Yu) is a traditional Chinese imperial-style dish in which a whole fish fillet — typically grass carp, snapper, or similar white-fleshed fish — is scored with deep cross-cuts so that when deep-fried, the flesh fans out into the shape of a blooming chrysanthemum flower. The fish is coated in a light cornstarch batter before frying, producing a golden, crispy exterior with a tender, moist interior. It is then finished with a glossy sweet and sour sauce made from sugar, vinegar, tomato ketchup, soy sauce, garlic, and ginger, giving the dish a bright, tangy-sweet flavour profile. The dish is visually spectacular and is associated with imperial Chinese banquet cuisine, where elaborate food presentation was a mark of culinary artistry. In Singapore, it is served in Chinese restaurants as a premium shared dish, often priced at SGD 60 or above for a restaurant-quality preparation. Nutritionally, the dish provides moderate protein from the fish (approximately 13.5 g per 100 g), moderate fat from deep-frying (approximately 8.5 g per 100 g), and significant carbohydrate and sugar from the sweet and sour sauce (approximately 16 g carbohydrate and 9.5 g sugar per 100 g). Sodium is moderately high at approximately 380 mg per 100 g. The dish has a low glycemic index (estimated 55) and a high glycemic load per standard serving, making portion control important for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or hypertension. The fish component contributes beneficial omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids, vitamin D, vitamin B12, and selenium. This dish is suitable for halal diets and is dairy-free, but contains fish and soy allergens and may contain gluten from the batter.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for chrysanthemum-shaped scored deep-fried fish with sweet and sour sauce exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA, or USDA). Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for deep-fried fish with USDA FoodData Central sweet and sour sauce composition in an approximate 70:30 ratio, adjusted for oil absorption. GI is an analogous estimate. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Food group corrected from 'Beverages' (input error) to 'Fish and seafood', which accurately reflects this deep-fried fish dish. 2. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy (deep-fried fish) and USDA FoodData Central (sweet and sour sauce) in a 70:30 ratio by weight, with adjustment for oil absorption during deep-frying; no exact approved-source entry exists for this specific dish. 3. GI of 55 (low) is an analogous estimate based on battered deep-fried fish with sweet and sour sauce; no direct GI measurement is available in the University of Sydney GI Database for this specific dish. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g (3.2), polyunsaturated_fat_g (2.8), trans_fat_g (0.1), potassium_mg (280.0), calcium_mg (25.0), iron_mg (0.8), cholesterol_mg (52.0), vitamin_c_mg (2.0), vitamin_a_mcg_rae (18.0), vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg (0.08), vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg (0.09), vitamin_b3_niacin_mg (3.2), vitamin_b6_mg (0.22), vitamin_b12_mcg (1.1), folate_mcg_dfe (12.0), vitamin_d_mcg (4.5), vitamin_e_mg (1.2), phosphorus_mg (185.0), magnesium_mg (28.0), zinc_mg (0.7), selenium_mcg (22.0), and water_g (58.0) are all estimated from USDA SR Legacy and USDA FoodData Central component data; these are not directly reported for this specific dish. 5. Halal status is assumed based on typical restaurant preparation with fish and plant-based sauce ingredients; individual restaurant certification should be verified. 6. The input record incorrectly classified this dish under 'Beverages'; this has been corrected. 7. Price of SGD 62.40 is noted from field survey data source (See Ya Tonight 今晚有局) but is not included as a nutritional field. 8.
